entry.title: Re: A report or feasible view is needed to view or analyz...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the classic report "Display Actual Cost Line Items – Sales Orders (App ID: KVBI)" is not available as it is only supported in the On-Premise and Private Cloud editions.

For Public Cloud users, the best alternative to analyze costing at the sales order line-item level is the Fiori app “Sales Volume – Profit Margin” (App ID: F0996).

This app provides:

  • Line-item level visibility into sales volume, cost of goods sold (COGS), and profit margin.
  • Drill-down by sales organization, product, or customer.
  • Gross margin KPIs directly on the Fiori launchpad.

You can access the app in your system or view it in the SAP Fiori Apps Library.

If further granularity is needed, consider using the Custom Analytical Queries app to create a tailored report that includes sales order line-item cost and margin data.

entry.title: SBC in ePPDS with CDP : No Process Selection Criteria popup
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently activating aATP SBC along with ePPDS and characteristic dependent planning as documented in this blog post (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-aatp-supply-creation-based-confirmation-sbc-series-part-4/ba-p/13565415).

According to the blog post and to some OSS notes I consulted, a popup called “Process Selection Criteria” should appear and ask for characteristics value for the batch determination. This is not happening and I have to manually trigger the batch determination that send me to the “usual old” SD determination screen:

If I modify the selection criteria in this screen, there are correctly taken into account into /SAPAPO/RRP3 to match with the expected batch, but this is not at all practical and this is misleading for users as no batch are selected in the sales order.

Steps already taken:

  • I have successfully activated the BC set switch /SAPAPO/APO_ON_ERP_SWITCHED
  • Configuration schema is set to CDP in SPRO
  • I have created a KMAT material with usual settings (300 class, profile, etc.) and configured a finished material in the plant data of the finished material.
  • I have created and assigned a class 023 to the finished material.
  • I have assigned organizational area to class and characteristics and created and activated integration models for these classes to create class 400 and 230
  • I have set up a checking group with PPAC check type and Supply Creation and assigned it to my finished material master
  • I have assigned a batch search strategy to sales order type and maintained required conditions records.

Any idea of why this is not working or where to debug this is welcome 🙂

Felipe Cadena

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Cannot allocate enough memory:Allocat...
entry.content: Perhaps you should check the memory in your DPAgent

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers
entry.content:
If you're a developer working in or around SAP, Sapphire can feel like drinking from a firehose of buzzwords. But if you zoom in a bit, this year had quite a bit that’s relevant if you build, extend, or operate on SAP.

This isn’t a recap of every announcement. Just the ones that I thought matter most to folks writing code, managing extensions, or figuring out how all this new AI stuff fits together. It was often the items that I found our team of Developer Advocates discussing in our back channel as we watched the Keynote together this morning.

AI Runtime, Prompt Optimization, and a Foundation for Builders

One of the more impactful announcements this year was the launch of the newly, updated AI Foundation. This isn’t a single tool, it’s a collection of services meant to help developers build, extend, and run AI more effectively across SAP. Personally, I'm looking forward to playing around with this soon. Maybe seeing it some of our upcoming CodeJams.

The most interesting piece of the AI Foundation might be the AI Agent Runtime in SAP BTP. It gives you a way to run AI agents natively, not as one-off scripts or sidecar tools. Alongside that, SAP introduced a Prompt Optimizer, which can take vague prompt ideas and turn them into tuned, testable input saving hours of trial and error. This also promises to make your prompts model agnostic, doing for AI what SAP has done for years when it comes to running applications on different operating systems.

Joule Studio, part of SAP Build, now supports skill and agent creation with low-code tools, hooks into SAP data, and context via the Knowledge Graph. You can bet we will see a whole more about Joule Studio later this year at SAP TechEd!

OSS Note 3437766: Availability of Generative AI Models

SAP Sapphire Innovation Guide 2025 | Business AI

Joule Agents: More Than a Copilot

Joule is evolving. This year, the story was about Joule Agents; modular, task-specific AI workers that will observe, reason, and act.

These agents are powered by SAP’s reasoning models, built on top of structured enterprise data, and connect across systems. They’re not stuck inside SAP thanks to Agent2Agent, a new protocol developed with Google, agents from different vendors can work together.

Full SAP Joule Agents announcement

Business Data Cloud: Structuring for AI at Scale

AI is only useful if it understands your data. That’s where SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) fits. It’s a harmonized foundation of curated, governed data built specifically to fuel AI-powered scenarios across SAP.

This year’s additions include:

  • Intelligent apps like People Intelligence and Spend Control Tower
  • A knowledge graph that gives agents and analytics the ability to understand data relationships
  • Availability across Google BigQuery, AWS, and Azure

Explore the full BDC announcement

Perplexity Partnership: Smarter Answers with a Wider Context

SAP and Perplexity AI are working together to bring a new kind of search-and-answer capability to Joule. The idea is to combine SAP's structured business data with trusted real-time external content to generate more complete, context-aware answers.

So, if you’re asking Joule about something happening within your business, you’re not limited to internal systems. Joule can pull in relevant external information from the web with full sourcing details.

Details on SAP + Perplexity partnership

SAP BTP: Better Tools, Cleaner Extensions

Plenty of updates to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) this year, most of them developer-relevant:

  • SAP Build is getting more capable. Joule Studio lets you build agents. New tools help you extend apps with less wiring.
  • AI-assisted ABAP tooling helps you migrate ECC code and align with clean core expectations. (See recent videos on SAP Developers Channel for examples).
  • Integration Suite adds predictive monitoring for APIs and an AI adapter that lets you slot in your own LLMs.
  • And finally, BTP services will be available through hyperscaler marketplaces like AWS and GCP later this year.

Full BTP update details

Final Thoughts

Sapphire 2025 sets the stage for TechEd 2025, naturally. If you want to get your hands-on with many of the announcements from today, then already start planning to attend TechEd or one of the TechEd on Tour events.

Some of the things we can look forward to trying out soon:

  • An AI runtime with tooling
  • Agentic AI that spans systems
  • A data layer built for AI use, not just reporting
  • BTP enhancements that feel aligned with how developers work

If you’re working on AI extensions, modernizing ABAP, or trying to get your data story right; this is the time to get ready to learn!

entry.title: How to create a new org unit without affecting the current set up in development customizing?
entry.content:
Knowing that after creating a organizational unit it cannot be deleted, would be possible to create a new organizational unit, like a company code, without affecting the current organizational unit?

For example, being able to test a new organization without affecting what is already set up in the development customizing tenant?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create a new org unit without affecting the cu...
entry.content:
Hello,

In a 3-system landscape this can be achieved using Parallel Line. The Parallel Line offers you a separate workspace in SAP Central Business Configuration and two additional tenants for configuration and testing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The main line focuses on maintaining innovation and keeping the productive environment up-to-date with SAP's latest features. With a separate parallel line, you can develop and test configuration enhancements without affecting the main line. The Parallel Line needs to be requested through " SAP for Me".

Please refer to Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal 

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Public View in S/4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

To identify the business catalog in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that allows you to save a view as public, you can utilize the SAP Fiori apps reference library. Here you can search and review the details associated with the apps and their respective business catalogs.

Steps to Find Business Catalog:

  1. Access SAP Fiori Apps Library: Go to SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library and select All apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud under the Categories section.
  2. Search for App Name or ID: Identify the app related to saving views (e.g., ‘Manage Views’, ‘View Settings’, etc.) in the search area.
  3. Check Implementation Information: Within the app details, go to the IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION tab and expand the Configuration section where you will find the required business catalogs.

If you still face difficulties, consult the Maintain Business Roles app to verify which business catalog contains the specific functionalities required for saving a view as public.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to Restrict WBS Element Usage in Sales Order Base...
entry.content:
Hello,

To address the need for restricting the assignment of WBS elements in the Sales Order creation process based on the project or project type in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can utilize the solution involving the use of custom logic in App Extensibility: WBS ElementBusiness Add-Ins (BAdIs).

Steps to Implement Custom Validation:

1. Utilize In-App Extensibility

SAP S/4HANA allows you to extend applications via In-App extensibility with custom logic. You can use the Custom Logic app to create validations.

2. Use Business Add-Ins (BAdIs)

  • Implement custom logic using the BAdIs available for Project Elements.
  • Two relevant BAdIs are available:
    • Project Element Validation - Enterprise Project: This BAdI allows you to validate values for properties of project elements such as WBS elements.
    • Project Element Determination - Enterprise Project: This can be used for determining values.

3. Code Example for Validation

Below is a conceptual example used to prevent project or work package release without budget allocation, which can be adapted for your requirement:

DATA(CURRENT_USER) = CL_ABAP_CONTEXT_INFO=>GET_USER_TECHNICAL_NAME().
SELECT SINGLE * FROM YY1_USERID INTO @DATA(LV_USER) WHERE USERDATA = @CURRENT_USER.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
IF ENTERPRISEPROJECTELEMENT-PROCESSINGSTATUS = '10'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGECLASS = '/S4PPM/VAL_DET'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGETYPE = 'E'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGENUMBER = '000'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGEVARIABLE1 = 'USER IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO RELEASE THE WORKPACKAGE'.
APPEND LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE TO VALIDATIONMESSAGES.
CLEAR LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

4. Configure Custom Fields

  • Use the Custom Fields app to create the necessary fields to distinguish between project types or IDs within your authorization logic.

Considerations

  • Ensure that the code within the BAdIs only runs for specific events or processing statuses that you are interested in.
  • Regularly test and debug custom logic in a non-production environment before deploying.

By using these custom validations, you can restrict WBS elements based on project scope effectively.

Help Resources

For further details, explore the following related resources: Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App

  • Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App
  • App Extensibility: WBS Element

This approach should help to enforce control over WBS element assignment based on your project’s specifics.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How can I make appear the internal price in the servi...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, pricing procedures help determine the final pricing for service quotations and service orders, but these do not automatically show internal costs. When managing costs related to a service that isn’t relevant to stock valuation, these are typically not reflected in the price details directly within service quotations or orders.

Steps to Reflect Costs:

1. Manual Price Entry

  • Use manual condition types like PMP0 to enter costs directly Pricing and Billing of Service Quotations in service quotations or orders.

2. Custom Pricing Procedures

  • If internal costs are vital for your pricing strategy, you may need to adjust the pricing procedures to include a custom condition type that represents internal pricing or costs. This involves the creation of condition tables and access sequences to directly associate these costs within the pricing methods.

3. Use of Service Quotation Quotation Types

  • Standard Service Quotations (SVQ1) and Fixed Price Service Quotations (SVQ2) allow you to handle items by fixed price or consumption-based billing. Ensure all pricing conditions are set to include relevant costs.

4. Training Sales Representatives

  • Educate sales reps to understand the process of manually inputting or verifying costs in quotations and services orders.

5. Set Custom Conditions Types

  • Utilize the SAP help documentation to assign custom condition types that can pick up non-stock related costs or manual entries.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Upgrade 1709 to SAP 2023 On-Premise from front/back end to embedded catalog
entry.content:
Does anyone know how to export/download catalogs and tiles?  I just need the Z* ones.   I can't seem to find anything.   The front-end system has been converted to 2023.   But we want to embed the system.   To do this all the catalogs and tiles have to be moved.   I can't seem to find anything on it.   And it seems like it would be a common request.  So I hate to ask.  I'll keep looking as well.  But any help would be appreciated.

Thank you!

Michelle

entry.title: Re: I want to understand how cost travel from sales order...
entry.content:
Hello,

To understand how cost transfers between a sales order and a purchase order, particularly in a 2-tier ERP system involving SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the scenario can be broken down into several steps.

Key Steps in the Cost Transfer Process

  1. Initialization: The subsidiary creates a purchase order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This order represents a request for goods or services.

  2. Using APIs: The purchase orders created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud are read using the API Purchase Order to Sales Order Creation in a 2-Tier… - SAP Community available at api.sap.com.

  3. Mapping to Sales Order IDOC: The purchase orders are mapped to a Sales Order IDOC (Intermediate Document) format suitable for transmission to an on-premise system at the headquarters.

  4. IDOC Transmission: The mapped IDOCs are then sent through the SAP Cloud Connector to the headquarters’ on-premise system.

  5. Processing at Headquarters: The headquarters processes the received IDOCs and converts them into sales orders.

  6. Cost Allocation: The cost associated with the purchase order is captured as the purchase turns into a sales order. This allows seamless integration of cost from procurement to sales, ensuring that all financials are in order as the transaction progresses from purchase to fulfillment.

Configuration

  • Communication arrangements between cloud and on-premise systems need to be set up to facilitate this workflow.
  • Partner Profiles and programming interfaces must be correctly configured at headquarters.

Additional Resources

  • For a detailed configuration guide on how communication between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud and on-premise systems for this purpose is set, you can refer to specific documentation available on SAP’s official site.

This process ensures that costs and other financial entries are accurately transferred and reconciled between the systems, leveraging both cloud capabilities and traditional on-premise infrastructure.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Release Standard Price in Valuation Typ 01 (Own Produ...
entry.content:
Hello,

When using split valuation and the standard price calculation, it’s crucial to ensure that the standard price is updated correctly across different valuation types. Here are the steps and considerations:

Steps to Release Standard Price to Valuation Type 1

  1. Ensure Proper Configuration:

    • In the material master record, double-check the settings for the valuation category and valuation type. Ensure that the valuation category ‘B’ (for procurement type) is correctly configured.

  2. Run a Cost Estimate:

    • Execute a standard cost estimate for the material. Ensure that this cost estimate is without errors.

  3. Mark and Release Standard Cost Estimate:

    • Use the app ‘Release Material Cost Estimates’ to mark and release Inventory Accounting - Marking and Releasing Standard Cost Estimates the standard cost estimate. When releasing, verify that the calculations reflect the required valuation type.

  4. Check Configuration for Split Valuation:

    • Verify in the configuration activities for split valuation that the standard price updates the valuation type accordingly. The valuation category Supply Chain - Valuation Category for split valuation must be adequately set to differentiate partial stocks.

  5. System Capabilities:

    • The SAP system should be set up to manage these ledger-specific material prices correctly by configuration in Manage Material Valuations or using specific APIs.

  6. Verify Period of Validity:

    • When releasing the standard cost estimate, ensure the validity period matches the current period in the material master to update correctly.

Troubleshooting

  • Check if Goods Receipt Based Invoice Verification is Defined: Access the purchase order, and ensure that goods receipt-based invoice verification is defined, allowing the system to handle partial deliveries separately.

  • Assess if Background Processing Matches Fiscal Settings: Make sure any existing background jobs are scheduled correctly, matching fiscal period requirements if you have variance in fiscal years.

Summary

Following these steps should help in updating the standard price for specific valuation types. It’s important to follow these considerations during the costing and release process to ensure the updates reflect accurately across all required valuation types.

For further details, you can refer to the documentation on marking and releasing standard cost estimates and split valuation Supply Chain - Creating Material Subject to Split Valuation configuration.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Questionmark cannot launch sample exam / I s this the only way?
entry.content:
Hi @Natalia1973 
Thank you for your engagement and posting this very important question. 
It looks like this is a new question.
I would like to encourage to used our SAP Learning Q&A section  and post new questions there.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses.
Would you mind to repost it and also provide more details regarding the issue you are facing.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: 免费销售订单，交货单过账时进费用科目
entry.content:
Hello,

To configure movement types for sales order schedule lines to ensure that delivery notes post to different accounts, you can follow these steps:

  1. Identify Movement Types: In SAP, movement types are three-digit identifiers that define the purpose of a goods movement, Supply Chain - The Movement Type Concept like a goods receipt or a goods issue. The movement type determines the accounting effects, such as which accounts are debited or credited.

  2. Link Movement Types to Schedule Lines: Ensure that the movement types are properly linked to the sales order schedule lines. This can be achieved by setting rules or configurations that determine the movement type for each schedule line based on specific criteria, like the nature of the transaction (e.g., customer relations vs. customer complaints).

  3. Configure Account Determination: Movement types can be associated with specific accounts by configuring account determination in SAP. This involves specifying the rules for which account each movement type posts to, based on the context of the transaction.

  4. Testing and Validation: Once configured, ensure to test the system by simulating transactions to validate that the correct accounts are used for different types of deliveries.

Example Implementation

In your specific scenario:

  • Customer Relations: Use a movement type configured to post to the sales expense account.
  • Customer Complaints: Use a different movement type that posts to the main business cost account.

These configurations allow automation in the attribution of costs to the correct accounts based on the type of sale or delivery involved.

SAP App for Movement Types

The SAP ‘Post Goods Movement’ app helps manage different types of goods movements, SAP Movement Types in SAP Cloud Public Edition including goods issues and receipts based on the movement type defined.

 

By utilizing the movement type concept efficiently, you can ensure accurate financial reporting and account management in your SAP system.

要配置销售订单计划行的移动类型，以确保送货单记入不同的账户，您可以按照以下步骤进行：

识别移动类型：在SAP中，移动类型是定义货物移动目的的三位数标识符，例如货物接收或货物发出。移动类型决定了会计影响，例如哪些账户被借记或贷记。
将移动类型链接到计划行：确保移动类型正确链接到销售订单计划行。这可以通过设置规则或配置来实现，根据特定标准（例如交易性质，如客户关系与客户投诉）确定每个计划行的移动类型。
配置账户确定：可以通过在SAP中配置账户确定来将移动类型与特定账户关联。这涉及到指定每个移动类型根据交易背景记入哪个账户的规则。
测试和验证：配置完成后，确保通过模拟交易测试系统，以验证不同类型的交付使用正确的账户。
具体场景实施示例：

客户关系：使用配置为记入销售费用账户的移动类型。
客户投诉：使用不同的移动类型记入主要业务成本账户。
这些配置允许根据销售或交付类型自动将成本归属到正确的账户。

SAP移动类型应用程序
SAP“货物移动过账”应用程序帮助管理不同类型的货物移动，包括基于定义的移动类型的货物发出和接收。

货物移动过账应用程序

通过有效利用移动类型概念，您可以确保SAP系统中的财务报告和账户管理的准确性。

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Hide People selector button in Goals. release 2025
entry.content:
Hello everyone, who is asking about translations for the Hide/Show People Selector, i found an answer in this KBA:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3607019

 

It says: "Note: These enhancements are patched to the 1H 2025 release, so translations of the labels 'Show/ Hide People Selector' won't be available until the 2H 2025 release. Moreover, the language key for these labels won't be available until the 2H 2025 release, so at this time there is no workaround to customize the labels. "

entry.title: Authorization Issue but the user already have the authorization on their profile
entry.content:
Hello gurus!

I'm facing a problem in which users have the necessary authorization to perform their function, but when they try to access it, they get an authorization error.

In su53 comes the object and values that the user already has in their role.

Roles are generated, they have the objects and values already requested in the role and it only works again when I redo the transport of that role.

If anyone can help me, I'd appreciate it!

Thank you,

Andre Macedo

entry.title: Condition type on pricing procedure
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I have created a condition type for the pricing procedure. The condition is to add shipping costs to my customer. However, I don't want these costs to change the unit price of the product in the line item. I just want them to be added at the end of the sales confirmation/invoice.

Thanks all fopr your help.

Best regards,

entry.title: AP invoices repository
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

My client is using the attachments in the manage supplier invoices app in 2 SL and would like to know how to access that repository. 

I believe documents are stored in SAP DMS or via ArchiveLink but i do not have much information on these. I have been told to use SAP_COM_0109 communication arrangements or connect via BTP-based content services for integration or backend access, unfortunately again i do not have much knowledge on that as well.

Any guidance on how to access that repository and a step by step guide will be greatly appreciated. 

thanks!

the system TRL is available again.

entry.title: Re: Multi VAT on single invoice line
entry.content: Thanks Abhijit, unfortunately this note talks about Ariba Network invoice interface with S/4 directly, but we have Ariba Invoicing layer between AN and S/4. First we need to bring Ariba network invoice to Ariba B&I, and this is where we are facing issue with tax code assignment.

entry.title: Re: Eliminate EC data freeze period during compensation p...
entry.content: Thanks a lot @xavierlegarrec.

entry.title: Re: post array using ODataV4 / V2
entry.content:
Hello,

if you can use oData V4, you can define a property as collection of Edm.String (Have a look at the answer there: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/post-array-in-single-field-via-odata-service/qaq-p/12485191).

I don't think it is possible to do it with oData V2 and SEGW in a "standard way". If you can change the incoming JSON format, you can stringify the array (or just use a comma separated string) and parse it on the ABAP side as workaround, or use an association to a new entity for "manufacturerCodes" (with the downside of needing a "code" property there, too).

entry.title: Re: Individual multiplier schemes for each assignment - V...
entry.content:
Hi @MeghaSadhotra 

Before putting this information which varies based on many criteria I would first try a VarPay configuration with Assignment Based Rating and one row per record where the ind_guideline defaults the right individual multiplier (based on all these criteria you mention) upon opening of the worksheets. This way you stick to a relatively standard configuration and also allow for possible overrides of the percentage.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hello @mlauber ,

Thanks a lot for your reply.

The system which I am working is on 2023 so release wise we are good.

I asked my basis team to check you queue config.

Here I have one more query, does RAP event trigger from normal GUI tcode as well, in this case VA01 ?

My client is asking irrespective of app(or EML) or GUI tcode, solution based on RAP event should work ,however, I am not sure RAP event would trigger from normal GUI tcode or not ?

Regards,

Jitendra

entry.title: SAC Analytics Designer - Formatting styling rules
entry.content:
Hello Sac experts!!

I have a table and apply a specific styling rule for the dimension Port. When the value is Port A it display blue colour on the column and when is Port B i display color green. However i would like to remove ou hide the line with Dimension Port. I've tried the code Table_1.removeDimension(' Port'), but the formatting rule didn't apply. Is there a way to make that happen?

 

Dimension DatePort APort BPort A
Dimension Port   
A503050
B402040
C101020

entry.title: Re: How many source objects we can add in one replication...
Re: Can't we use the same source objects if it is already...
entry.content: replication flow listens to the source object. therefore only one flow can be the listener

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Cannot allocate enough memory:Allocat...
entry.content:
I would persist the model-view.

If it fails on OoM you can:

#create partitions (f.e. year, ...) and persist each partition

#persist sub-views until you do not get the OoM

entry.title: Re: Invalid manager message with off cycle event batch
entry.content:
We are having a similar issue where the off cycle event isn't processed when the employee's manager is inactive. The error message received is: The manager provided in the record has the status Terminated which means they are inactive.

The employee's record should be processed regardless of their manager's status, but doesn't.

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Management in SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content: Thank you for your feedback, Chuma. Could you please share the related processes, as I’m unable to find the app “Manage Landed Costs”? Thank you in advance .

entry.title: Re: How to create a dynamic offset variable in SAP Datasp...
entry.content: You have to do this in front-end. I use scripting in SAC directly. Catch the entered value and use it to either directly filter the datasource() or via range variable

entry.title: Crystal Reports 2025 trial key?
entry.content:
Hi!

I just downloadd Crystal Reports 2025 to see if the upgrade is worth it, but where do I get the trial key? Upon startup it asks for a key, but I cannot see any key?

entry.title: Re: Physical Inventory Process in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi @GiuliaGisc 

Partial counting in a bin via RF is possible, and you do not need to count all lines at once. But the inventory document will only be considered complete when all relevant lines are counted and confirmed.

Thanks,

Prasanna

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2025 trial key?
entry.content: Ah... The key was to wait. The email with the key arrived much later. No idea if it was timing, or that I finally managed to install it. Hint: Knowledgebase article 1730976

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Management in SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

My apologies for the misunderstanding

The "Manage Landed Costs" app is not a standard application. Managing landed costs typically involves manual processes. After goods receipt, additional costs such as freight or customs duties are handled by creating non-PO-based supplier invoices. These costs are then manually allocated to the relevant inventory items to adjust their valuation accordingly

Thanks and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Sesh_Sreenivas 
Thanks for sharing this. Given a 3 system landscape (Dev, QA, Prod), where is this function intended to be used? I assume it is for Dev, and the artifacts need to be transported. Is this understanding correct?

entry.title: Re: Individual multiplier schemes for each assignment - V...
entry.content: Hi Xavier, Thank you for suggesting this. I tried but it doesn't work as intended. The individual multiplier for each assignment still defaults to the matching criteria of the latest record (job level and code after promotion and effective 31st Dec) which is available on the Summary row. It does not take into account the previous record where Job level and Job Code were different (prior to promotion). As a result, for all the two assignments, Individual multiplier is same instead of two different values. Do you think I am missing something here?

entry.title: Re: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to producit
entry.content:
@TobiasAdlereverything is working as expected. Thanks for the reply. One additional question i have is on proposing the Source HU as the destination HU in the case of storage type being batch-neutal.
1. We have the storage type set to let the wwarehouse Process Type Controls Proposal for Destination HU

 

2. The process type it set for rounding and Proposing Source HU as destination HU 

During the RF picking and Placement to the PSA the HU is lost the destination HU not diplayed on the screen and is greyed out.

 

However if you were to select the batch on task in the web it will use the source as the destination. This seems strange will work in the UX but in the RF.

Is this possible in the RF in Batch-Neutral. FYI there is only 1 batch of the same product on the HU and we are picking the entire HU. There is no mixed batches.

@TobiasAdler

entry.title: Re: /XNFE/COLLECT_DOCUMENTS
entry.content: Muito bom, funcionou aqui perfeitamente e a nota saiu de resumo preliminar.

entry.title: Supplier/vendor Evaluation Process in SAP S/4HANA with Questionnaire Guide
entry.content:
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in Supplier Evaluation

The supplier evaluation system in SAP HANA incorporates both hard facts and soft facts. For the hard facts, you need to specify the criteria weightings and assign scores through Customising or via the SAP Fiori app Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring.

To select and weight the criteria, choose the app Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring.

Note: After changing the scores, you must run the report MM_PUR_SEWS_VAR_SCORE_UPDATE to recalculate the scores.

The following key performance indicators (KPIs) are available for analysis:

1. Overall Supplier Evaluation: This app evaluates a supplier's performance within an organisation by analysing both operational and questionnaire-based scores. The assessment covers data from the previous year up to the present date. It calculates the overall score by combining weighted hard metrics (such as price, delivery time, and quantity) with soft metrics derived from questionnaire responses.

2. Operational Supplier Evaluation: This app calculates a supplier's performance score within an organisation by taking the weighted average of three key metrics: quantity variance, price variance, and time variance. The score is derived from data collected over the past year up to the current date. The overall score is computed by first assessing variances across these criteria and then applying the appropriate weighting. If needed, you can modify the scores using the Adjust Operational Supplier Evaluation Score app.

3. Supplier Evaluation by Quantity: The Supplier Evaluation by Quantity app helps you calculate quantity variance by comparing the ordered quantity with the delivered quantity. Both over-deliveries and under-deliveries are counted as variances. The score is calculated based on data from the previous year up to the current date.

4. Supplier Evaluation by Time: This app helps you evaluate a supplier's performance score by analysing the time difference between when items were ordered and when they were delivered. The score is calculated based on data from the previous year to date, considering both early and late deliveries as variances. You can also track delivery time variances by date, with each day's variance counted as one percent.

5. Supplier Evaluation by Price: With the Supplier Evaluation by Price app, you can determine the score of a given supplier in an organisation based on the variance in the price of ordered items and invoiced items. The score is calculated over the previous year to date and is based on the difference between the purchase order amount and the invoice amount. Both the excess price and the lower price are considered as variances. This is completely different to the LIS-based supplier evaluation, where the price score is based on the info record prices.

6. Supplier Evaluation by Quality - Inspection Lot: The Supplier Evaluation by Quality app helps you calculate a supplier’s overall quality score using inspection data. It records each inspection lot and tracks suppliers by purchase order and item. The score is calculated based on data from the previous year up to the current date.

7. Supplier Evaluation by Quality - Quality Notification: This app allows you to assess suppliers using a quality-based scoring system determined by the number of complaints received. You can easily identify high-performing suppliers - those with fewer complaints and higher quality scores - helping you determine the most reliable partners. Additionally, if purchasing conditions like material quality, price, quotation, and delivery time are similar, the app assists in selecting the best supplier based on their performance metrics.

Questionnaire

Supplier Evaluation by Questionnaire App

This app allows you to assess supplier performance by calculating evaluation scores based on completed questionnaires. You can compare the actual scores with the supplier’s target score to measure performance. Evaluations are based on data from the previous year up to the current date.

Prerequisites for Evaluation

Before starting the evaluation process, you need to set up questions and questionnaires.

1. Manage Questions App

Use this app to define all questions that will be included in your questionnaires. Follow these steps:

  • Define Groups (Optional): Organise questions into logical sections for better structure
  • Create Sections: Group questions by topic for easier navigation
  • Add Questions: Specify questions and assign them to groups if needed. You can choose from different question types. For single-answer and yes/no questions, you must assign scores to each answer option

2. Manage Questionnaires App

Once your questions are ready, use this app to build questionnaires. You can link questionnaires to purchasing categories and structure them with sections and questions.

Step by Step Process:

(Maintain the Number Ranges for the Purchasing Category in the path below 🙂

SPRO -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Supplier and Category Management -> Purchasing Categories -> Define Number Range for Purchasing Categories

SPRO -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Supplier and Category Management -> Supplier Evaluation -> Number Ranges for Supplier Evaluation -> Define Number Range for Questions / Define Number Range for Sections / Define Number Range for Question and Section Groups / Define Number Range for Evaluation Questionnaires / Define Number Range for Supplier Evaluation Templates / Define Number Range for Supplier Evaluation Requests / Define Number Range for Evaluation Responses / Define Number Range for Evaluation Scorecards 

Step 1: Create Purchasing Category (App: Manage Purchasing Categories)

Click Create

Mention Purchasing Category Name, add the suppliers, and the internal team, etc.

Step 2: Supplier Evaluation and Scoring (App: Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring)

Click Create

Set the Weighting percentage, and maintain the weighting factors for each Criterion as per your requirements.

Quantity

Time

Price

Save the data

Step 3: Open the Manage Questions Evaluation App (App: Manage Questions)

Pre-requisite config: Create Question Library in the original language in Customization in the below path

SPRO -> MM -> Purchasing -> Supplier and Category Management -> Supplier Evaluation -> Basic Settings for Supplier Evaluation -> Create Question Library for Supplier Evaluation

Click Section, New and mention Section Details.

Once saved, navigate to the Questions tab and create questions as per your requirements.

Yes/No Question

Single Answer Question allows the recipient to mention comments.

Mention the score for each answer in the Answers tab.

Step 4: Manage Questionnaire (App: Manage Evaluation Questionnaires) 

Click Create

Mention the Questionnaire name, the person responsible and assign Purchasing Category (Several purchasing categories can be assigned, Not only one).

Navigate to the Questions tab, Maintain Overall Target and Overall Minimum Score and add the section that was created in the previous step.

 

Select the questions 

You can set a question to disqualify suppliers based on their response by selecting the appropriate checkbox. This means that if the answer given to the question does not meet your expectations, the supplier is disqualified. The person answering the question does not see whether a question is disqualifying or not.

If the respondent gives a disqualifying answer, this is indicated in the evaluation or qualifying response. This indicator is only for your information and has no direct consequences on the status of the supplier.

Note: Only Yes/No questions can be marked as disqualified.

Click Release

Step 5: Manage Evaluation Templates (App: Manage Evaluation Templates)

The Manage Templates app lets you create reusable templates for evaluation requests with shared properties, like questionnaires. In group-based evaluations, templates are set up for supplier peer groups within specific purchasing categories, assigning both suppliers and appraisers. Templates include questionnaires, response deadlines, and texts. When generating a request, template data is copied—only deadlines and texts can be modified, not the questionnaires.

(Appraiser: An appraiser is a person responsible for evaluating and scoring a supplier’s performance using established criteria. They gather feedback, typically through questionnaires or interviews, and their evaluations are consolidated into a supplier scorecard.)

Click Create

Navigate to Appraisers, mention the Evaluation Template Name, Response deadline, and Purchasing Category.

Maintain the Appraisers.

(Appraiser: An appraiser is a person responsible for evaluating and scoring a supplier’s performance using established criteria. They gather feedback, typically through questionnaires or interviews, and their evaluations are consolidated into a supplier scorecard.)

Navigate to the Questionnaires tab and add the questionnaire that was created in the previous step. 

Add the Suppliers under the Supplier Peer Group for which the Evaluation is to be performed. 

Click Release

Click Start Group-Based Evaluation

Mention the Evaluation Request name, the Period to be evaluated, and click Submit Evaluation Request

 

Step 6: Evaluate Supplier (App: Evaluate Suppliers)

The supplier's list appeared here for evaluation, that are assigned to you.

Click on each supplier and evaluate them

Once after submission, the Overall score will be displayed according to the criteria and weighting set in the previous steps.

Similarly, perform the evaluation process for other suppliers, finally, the status will be changed to Submitted.

Step 7: Display Score Cards (Display Scorecards)

The Display Scorecards app shows a supplier's overall evaluation results from their scorecard. Each supplier and evaluation request has one scorecard. For example, yearly evaluations create separate scorecards per year. To view all scorecards for a supplier, use the evaluation template.

Target Score, Total Score, and Minimum Score are displayed for each supplier.

Click on the line item to see the detailed scorecard.

Step 8: Supplier Evaluation by Questionnaire (App: Supplier Evaluation By Questionnaire)

The Supplier Evaluation by Questionnaire app calculates supplier scores from questionnaires and lets you compare them against target scores.

View By Supplier, click on settings, select the views to Questionnaire Score, Minimum Score, and number of responses.

See by Document, Purchasing Category, and By Supplier.

 

Regards,

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: Re: Error Handling and Reprocessing of Odata through Application Interface Framework (AIF) Tool
entry.content: for S4HANA 2023 private editino, when we try to create service using SEGW we get error "Object type IWPR is not supported in ABAP Cloud"

entry.title: Re: Disable Windows Authentication on Data Source in RPT...
entry.content:
To explain why this is happening is as follows....

Using the basic SetLogon info in code uses what's saved in the RPT file, all you are actually doing is setting the user name and password, the rest is loaded from the Report when designed.

In short the only way to removed the Trusted Authentication is to manually open each report in the Designer  and using Set Location under the Database menu to a new DSN, use the ones you have on the Server, that DSN must be on the PC where you updated the Reports, then Verify the Reports in the Designer and save them to a new location. Then use those in the WEB app.

To actually update the connection info you need to use ReplaceConnection() which is available using RAS. RAS does exist for your use with nothing special to install, it does require code changes though so you will need to get a Developer involved to make the change.

Also, SP 21 is quite old and you should upgrade the runtime to SP 37 which is the latest release, but again it will require a developer to recompile the app to use the updated SP. 

To get the latest go here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

On that page there is a sample app called:

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

It's a desk top app but the same code can be used in a WEB app. If you search for ReplaceConnection you'll see how it's done. In short here's the work flow:

Open the RPT file using the Engine

Convert the Report to a RAS Report Object

Use ReplaceConnection to update the Connection info and set the Trusted to False.

Save the report.

If you don't have many reports you may want to update each report manually using the Designer.

Also, if you should ask the Developer or maker of the WEB app to give you an updated version that uses the latest SP 37 runtime and at the same time allow you to update the DB connection info using RAS.

Have fun

Don

entry.title: Re: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to producit
entry.content:
following this thread as this answer is important for a project I'm on as well

@TobiasAdler

entry.title: Re: US Bonus tax depreciation key setup
entry.content: As of 05/2025 - Per Notes 2587054, 3362553, 3502413, and 3326552 a standard solution to the applying special bonus depreciation retroactively if your fiscal vs. calendar years are different is not supported by SAP S4 HANA. These notes provide a sequence of steps for workarounds with risks and possibilities of changing closed years values that should remain constant, and disclaimers that the use of these steps is at the company’s risk and that the results should be validated and adjusted by your company and/or SAP consultants to meet requirements. Disclaimers: “If your fiscal year differs from the calendar year, you must adjust the multilevel method so that the percentages and the tax year match.” “This SAP Note provides only the technical possibility of how to achieve an immediate tax writeoff using a depreciation key. The tax aspects and accounting aspects must be clarified in your company.” “If you call the program, you must first confirm that you have read and understood this SAP Note. Therefore, go through this SAP Note with your consultant…” 3326552 - Post back to closed fiscal years in S/4 3502413 - Paradoxical situation because depreciation start date in past closed fiscal year 2587054 - Special depreciation USA: Tax Cuts and Job Act 2017 3362553 - Special depreciation USA for the years 2023 - 2026

entry.title: Create Email Activity from Cloud Application Studio with rich text and embedded images
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement on SAP Business ByDesign to output Email Activites with rich text (html) and embedded images.

We have accomplished the html part (following this guide: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/html-custom-email-notifications-in-sap-cloud-applications-studio/ba-p/13084626).

To handle images, I expect we need to add the images as attachments (this is easy and then they go out as part of the multipart message and become attachments to the email) but how do I link it up so an image shows in the body text (i.e. referenced in the html) rather than as an attachment?

Thanks in advance!

//Carl

entry.title: Introducing PDF Export of Requirements in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In your SAP Implementation projects, it is essential to consolidate and communicate requirement related data in a clear, structured format is. With the new PDF Export functionality, teams can easily generate documentation of their requirements and the contributing project artifacts.

Introduction:

 The Requirement Traceability app helps project teams visualize and track the lifecycle of requirements and their related entities. It provides end-to-end visibility, ensuring alignment across the implementation journey: from planning to testing and defect resolution.

With the new PDF Export feature, you can now generate comprehensive document/report that captures the relationship and progress of requirements and their associated artifacts for :

  • Storing a locked version of the requirements for data holding purpose
  • Attaching requirements to contract documents or in a fixed price project
  • Fulfilling in customer specific documentation rules to bring requirement specific data to documentation environment
  • Stakeholder communication,
  • Unaltered Documentation (Steering committee meetings or project status reviews)
  • Regulatory or quality assurance audits
  • Knowledge transfer between project phases or teams
  • Backups of requirement sets at key milestone
  • Time stamped records 

What Gets Exported?

The PDF report includes:

  • Project Information
  • Who and when exported it
  • Filter applied information: Requirement identifiers such as status and other filters selected, Indirect Assignments and linking objects
  • Linked artifacts: assigned user stories, test cases, features, and defects with key attributes

How to Export Requirements in PDF Format

Exporting requirements in PDF format from SAP Cloud ALM is straightforward:

  1. Navigate to Requirement Traceability: navigate to the Requirement Traceability 

     

  2. Select Requirements: You can select individual requirements requirements you want to export.

     

  3. Generate PDF: Click the export button to generate the PDF file. The system will process to create a PDF document containing the selected requirements with a Cover Page that contatins the project name and details of person who exported the document and when.

Alternatively:

  1. Apply local filters in Traceability Application: such as specific requirement status, scopes, Solution Process, Tags etc.
  2. Toggle indirect assignments: If you turn this on for Test cases and Defects, the app includes not only directly linked items but also those indirectly associated through intermediary links.

3. Export to PDF : The exported document now captures these exact filter settings, including indirectly assigned test cases and defects with a mention of the linking object in the relationship table.

This means the PDF isn’t just a static snapshot—it’s a reproducible view. Anyone who receives the document can understand exactly what was included in the export and recreate the same view in the application using the same filters.

This new enhancement bridges the gap between digital workflows and offline documentation. More enhancements such as Requirement description and change history are on their way. Stay tuned for more insights into PDF export functionality in SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade 1709 to SAP 2023 On-Premise from front/back e...
entry.content:
Short answer: bundle them in a transport request and ask basis to export the request in the source system and import in the target system.

Long answer:

That depends which kind of catalogs you have and which ones you need to migrate / or the ones which does make sense.

Standard Catalogs

Catalogs in the past (up until version 2022 if I am not mistaken) were stored as Web Dynpro Component Configuration Objects (R3TR WDCC) - at least the ones created in the Configuration Layer (in Launchpad Designer, CONF scope i.e. using tcode /UI2/FLPD_CONF). The ones created in the Customizing Layer are likely stored in a table which I do not remember by heart now. A transport of copies should allow you do store both.

It's possible to identify the Z* in SE85 by searching for Web Dynpro > Component Configurations and using the catalogs/group ID which is visible in the launchpad designer using the "short text/description" field when searching. Make sure to use the original language of the catalogs/groups when using SE85. WDCC IDs are like UUIDs.

Remote Catalogs

If you had Back end catalogs (similar to the SAP*BE_APPS) created in Fiori Launchpad App manager (tcode /UI2/FLPAM) created in the back-end they were replicated to the front end system. In this case no migration is needed but you will need you replicate them in the back-end system assuming it became the embedded instance. Catalogs created using the Fiori Launchpad App Manager are not stored anymore as WDCC.

Bear in mind Technical catalogs should be created in application manager in 2023 so you might transport your technical catalogs and them migrate them to this new formar. (program /UI2/MIGRATION - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migr... - SAP Community)

Other Catalogs

Other catalogs types are obsolete so it should not worth migrating them

Groups

Groups have are also obsolete in 2023 so Spaces and Pages should be used. If you have good reasons for still using it, they are stored just like standard catalogs (WDCC)

entry.title: Re: Bonus depreciation key with 50% first year
entry.content:
As of 05/2025 - Per Notes 2587054, 3362553, 3502413, and 3326552 a standard solution to the applying special bonus depreciation retroactively if your fiscal vs. calendar years are different is not supported by SAP S4 HANA.  If your fiscal vs. calendar year is the same, the steps provided in these notes may work for your situation.

These notes provide a sequence of steps for workarounds with risks and possibilities of changing closed years values that should remain constant, and disclaimers that the use of these steps is at the company’s risk and that the results should be validated and adjusted by your company and/or SAP consultants to meet requirements.

Disclaimers:

“If your fiscal year differs from the calendar year, you must adjust the multilevel method so that the percentages and the tax year match.”

“This SAP Note provides only the technical possibility of how to achieve an immediate tax writeoff using a depreciation key. The tax aspects and accounting aspects must be clarified in your company.”

“If you call the program, you must first confirm that you have read and understood this SAP Note. Therefore, go through this SAP Note with your consultant…”

3326552 - Post back to closed fiscal years in S/4

3502413 - Paradoxical situation because depreciation start date in past closed fiscal year

2587054 - Special depreciation USA: Tax Cuts and Job Act 2017

3362553 - Special depreciation USA for the years 2023 - 2026

entry.title: Re: AS91 Special/bonus depreciation fields grayed out.
entry.content:
A note providing a solution to address this issue was created in 10/2024:

2676325 - During creating Legacy asset via transaction AS91, field 'Cumulative Special Depreciation' greyed out

entry.title: Re: post array using ODataV4 / V2
entry.content: Thanks for your time. Just to mention in OdataV4 I don't any option to define the property as collection as the other blog mention.

entry.title: Re: post array using ODataV4 / V2
entry.content: The collection is only available for navigation properties.

entry.title: Consuming parameterized views in Fiori Elements oData v4
entry.content:
Hi,

We need to consume a parameterized view in CAP Node.js (which under the hood is a HANA Calculation View) in a Fiori Elements for oData v4 "AnalyticalTable" application.

We find documentation for how to do this in oData v2: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/8e3b6be7128344e9bdcfceb3219f0de7

XML based annotations here: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/4bd7590569c74c61a0124c6e370030f6.html#loio4bd7590569c74c61a0124c6e370030f6/suppprting_parameterized_entities_subsection

ABAP CDS examples here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-fiori-list-report-utilizing-cds-view-with-parameters/ba-p/13500626

But we do not find documentation, guides or examples for how to do that with CAP. The view looks like this

@CDS.persistence.exists
@cds.persistence.calcview
entity MyView(IP_MYPARAM::MY_PARAM) {
    @analytics.Dimension: true
    key MyDimension : String;
    @analytics.Measure: true
    @Aggregation.default: #SUM
    MyMeasure : Decimal;
}

Does anyone have a working sample or other guidance?

Thanks in advance!

//Carl

entry.title: Re: Credit Memo amounts are not getting reduced in Delta...
entry.content:
Hi @Debarati6 ,

Try the below configuration. It might help you.

In the node 'Specify Settlement Settings for the Condition Contracts", under the "Settlement" section, you will find the field "Save Detailed Statement Data". Change this field to Y or Z and then check.

Regards

Hari

entry.title: Re: Individual multiplier schemes for each assignment - V...
entry.content: I think something must be off in your configuration because the purpose of ABR is to be able to enter different recommendations (as well as guidelines) for different employee history records the employee has.

entry.title: Re: How does your business manage items like first aid an...
entry.content:
Hi @Sarah_Rothery 

I've used Curricula Retraining to manage license renewals for several large organizations in Western Australia, including those in healthcare and the mining and resources sectors.

While it's not a perfect solution—particularly for licenses like the MSIC, which can have 2-, 3-, or 5-year renewal periods, or temporary High Risk Work Licenses (HRWL) that expire after just three months—we've adopted a consistent approach. We align all licenses to a 5-year renewal cycle by recording the start date accordingly and noting the actual expiry details in the Learning History Record.

I understand that enabling end dates for items would require a significant overhaul of the LMS functionality, but it would be a valuable enhancement for managing compliance more effectively.

Please feel free to reach out via email or message to discuss, as I'm sure that I have come across similar challenges and may be able to offer potential solutions or ideas.

Best regards,
Nathan H.

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Payment Times Report - Statutory Reporting
entry.content:
Executing payment times report, but currently, SAP only generates the Payment times report (PTR) in csv format. Is there any way to obtain the responsible member declaration template from SAP? If not, it is possible to include this in the existing functionality?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Payment Times Report - Statutory Reporting
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The statutory reporting relies on tax data, as all applicable taxes must be submitted to the tax authorities. The legal file generated when running the report follows a standard format defined by the tax authority or government

As per statutory reporting guidelines, report is created based upon the tax authority provided format. Please use the standard report which is delivered by SAP.

 

If a different template or format is used, the generated file will not be accepted by the tax authority.

 

Please refer the below help document supported compliance tasks by country/region for statutory reporting (Including with Payment time report),

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/71af4585db6d4904b1724730f3776c9b/097165e9c1074038847625e5c53e07d2.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Case Management with Sales order related
entry.content:
Hello Experts

We are currently working with SAP Services Cloud Version 2 and we are using the case management funciontality. is there a way to create a case taking the initial information from a sales order that was created in S4hana and has been replicated in SAP Services? or is the case only created related to the customer?

entry.title: Re: List with 16 SAP S/4 HANA Fiori Apps for Analysis and Reports | Sourcing and Procurement (MM)
entry.content: very insightful

entry.title: Re: ABAP Runtime Error in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2-Sys...
entry.content: For your information, we are too on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with a 2-system landscape and we have the same problem since sunday. no transactions, no billing and no good issue on outbound delivery works. we made case since this morning, but no resolve by SAP at this time.

entry.title: Re: SAP Process Automation: How to test the API trigger with postman
entry.content:
Hello Expert, I followed the Video 3, Postman request is successful (Status Code: 201), but it does not show in the My Inbox. I can see the Request in the Build -> Monitoring -> Process and Workflow Instances. -> Logs seems fine, no errors and How do i debug and What i am doing wrong? @peterengel 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wn-mxCqPB6k 

API Scenario requests are not showing in My Inbox. Form Requests shows fine, i can approve / reject. only API requests are not showing up in the App. I tried to execute the Network call with no filters, but no luck.

entry.title: Re: Changes in SAP GUI 800 Patch 5+ Affecting RPA Automation (Windows)
entry.content: Hi AbhishekDubey, Removing test automation tool tag as this is only for testing tools in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: My Inbox PO Link Opens ME23N Instead of Fiori App "Ma...
entry.content: Dear @noobs, Would you please find more details in the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002822661? This is due to the PO is created with some features which are not supported in the Fiori app 'Manage Purchase Orders', so it will be displayed with me23n.

entry.title: Re: My Inbox PO Link Opens ME23N Instead of Fiori App "Ma...
entry.content: Dear @noobs, Would you please find more details from the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0002822661? This is because the impacted POs were create with features are not supported in Fiori app, so these POs will be displayed in app me23n.

entry.title: Re: Hard code bill to address for a PO
entry.content: Dear @ Shama321, by mentioning the bill to address, do you mean the partner in the header level?

entry.title: Re: How to Configure EAN 13 and EAN 14 Codes with External Number Assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Pu
entry.content: Dear @Katalina_Guio, by mentioning the EAN 13 Code, would you please further advise in which object will these codes be assigned? Is that the material master data level? Or in the PO level?

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud - 無法copy Purchase Requisition Header...
entry.content: Dear @yannichan, Would you please find more details from KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003587283?

entry.title: Re: Notification for the released parked invoice in SAP S4hana public cloud
entry.content: Notifiy expert to check this thread.

entry.title: Enhancing VIM fiori app Gateway service
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I am trying to check for the feasiblity to enhance the gateway service for Vendor Invoice management application provided by OpenText. The gateway service is PF05_DATA. I have run through a few posts but there is not enough information that helps me to understand if this gateway service can be enhanced. I could only get the service name and the data provider class but could not see a provision to enhance. I could not figure out the BSP application associated with the app nor able to find in the data provider class where the data is retrieved from.

entry.title: Re: Notification for the released parked invoice in SAP S...
entry.content:
Hi, this can be achieved by email template configuration in app Maintain Email Templates. For detailed information please refer to this online documentation, section "Optional: Maintain Email Templates": https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ff92035b3da94a14a3d2d0faffad564c.html?locale=en-US

  • Email notification for workflow initiators in case of workflow approval:
    Copy the predelivered email template SWF_WORKFLOW_COMPLETE_NOTIFY into your namespace. The custom template must follow this pattern: YY1__COMPLETE_POSITIVE.
    • Workflow for Blocked Invoice
      So please use: YY1_00800251_COMPLETE_POSITIVE.
    • Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete
      So please use: YY1_00800303_COMPLETE_POSITIVE.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Configuration in SAP Integration Suite: One Tenant, Multiple Environments
entry.content: please replicate the iflows so that the dev work won't impact the test.

entry.title: The requested end date in service order can't be changed
entry.content: The requested end date will always be set to three days later than the posting date (requested start date) automatically, and can't be changed.

entry.title: Service Entry Sheet Tolerance
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a Service PO with qty 1 & net Price 5000. Than I created a SES for Qty 1 & Value $237.

The invoice comes in for $237.35. When I post this invoice the invoice does not post as it is imbalanced by 0.35 cents.

I want the SES/Invoice to have a tolerance of $250/- only. So that any invoice that has a variance of $250/- it is posted insted of going into exception handling.

Please advise if there is any other tolerance configuration for SES  apart from OMR6 PP Key.

Thanks

Anuj

entry.title: Re: ABAP Adaptation for Any Middleware to CPI/IS Migration ( RFC to Webservice )
entry.content: Nice blog post Hasini, listing down all the needed steps in detail. Good work !
